<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
All required note files have been read. Ready to compile the requested table.

The "when" clause follows a first-person tradeoff pattern from the jorap-voice skill. Some files contain multiple relationships per line, such as Extends and Contradicts together.

Only the Contradicts portion of each line will receive the new when clause; the rest of the line stays intact. Drafting of the suggested phrases has begun, starting with capture.md.

Drafted the first three suggestions. Capture.md contradicts Signal vs Noise when the user captures first and skips filtering. Signal vs Noise.md contradicts RSS for Research when curated feeds are needed despite most entries staying unread. PKM.md contradicts The Collector's Fallacy when the system tempts hoarding without express intent. Building-a-second-brain.md contradicts The Collector's Fallacy too.

Building-a-second-brain.md now has its when clause: when CODE steps become collection theater without express intent. The-collectors-fallacy.md contradicts Capture when hoarding feels like progress and saves are skipped. Creative-output.md contradicts The Collector's Fallacy when shipping pressure makes me skip saves I will actually use.

Active-knowledge-curation.md contradicts The Collector's Fallacy when curation becomes another form of collecting without output. The "when" clause is being refined to explain the tradeoff from the perspective of this note against the contradicted one.

Reconsidering the direction of the tradeoff. The "when" clause should explain when the contradicted concept applies, not when this note pushes against it. This reverses the earlier assumption.

Capture.md contradicts Signal vs Noise when the this-month filter talks me out of saving a spark I'll need later. The Eternal Principles example confirms the tradeoff runs from this note against the contradicted one.

The "when" clause should describe the situation where the contradicted note's wisdom applies or where this note breaks down. The take-up-your-cross example clarifies this: it contradicts "There Is No Perfect Solution" when cost-free discipleship is expected.

Capture.md contradicts Signal vs Noise when resonance beats the this-month filter. Signal vs Noise.md contradicts RSS for Research when strict filtering clashes with subscribing to feeds.

RSS for Research now suggests "when curated feeds beat one-off saves." PKM.md suggests "when the system itself feels like progress without output." Building-a-second-brain.md now uses "when organize and distill become hoarding without express."

Collector's Fallacy now suggests "when skipping the save kills a spark I'll ship from." Creative Output versus Collector's Fallacy suggests "when output pressure makes me skip capture I'd need for the draft."

Creative Output versus Collector's Fallacy is refined to "when output pressure makes me skip capture I'd need to ship." Active Knowledge Curation versus Collector's Fallacy now suggests "when monthly pruning replaces actually shipping."

Atomic Notes contradicts Maps of Content when a hub page merges ideas instead of keeping one claim per file. Associative Linking contradicts PARA Method when folder structure beats waiting for links to emerge.

PARA Method versus Associative Linking now suggests "when life buckets beat waiting for links to find each other." The inverse relationship suggests "when topic links beat sorting by owner and deadline." Note Relationships versus PARA Method suggests "when typed links beat sorting by project folder." Maps of Content versus Atomic Notes suggests "when an index gathers claims that should stay atomic."

Graph View Analytics versus Maps of Content suggests "when link counts beat a hand-curated index." Evergreen Notes versus Daily Notes suggests "when today's scratch beats two-year phrasing." Daily Notes versus Evergreen Notes suggests "when fleeting capture beats polishing for permanence."

Two-speeds framework versus evergreen-only polish suggests "when both lanes beat forcing everything evergreen." Knowledge lifecycle versus evergreen notes suggests "when stage-matched effort beats evergreen polish on every note." Progressive summarization versus synthesis as a goal suggests "when layers beat rewriting into what I believe."

Synthesis as a Goal versus Progressive Summarization suggests "when merged belief beats layered highlights on borrowed words." Intellectual Sourcing versus Progressive Summarization suggests "when citations beat distill-on-demand layers." Spaced Repetition versus Progressive Summarization suggests "when drill schedules beat bold layers for recall."

Feynman Technique versus Spaced Repetition suggests "when explaining gaps beats flashcard drill." Pareto Principle versus Compounding suggests "when cutting the long tail kills compounding habits." Compounding versus Pareto Principle suggests "when boring repetition beats protecting only the vital few."

Minimum Effective Dose versus Compounding suggests "when enough-to-work beats stacking past the dose." Low Hanging Fruit versus Compounding suggests "when the easy win today beats waiting for the curve." Process Over Outcomes versus The 12 Week Year is now being drafted.

Process Over Outcomes versus The 12 Week Year now suggests "when the daily process beats quarterly scoreboard pressure." There Is No Perfect Solution versus Pareto Principle suggests "when good-enough tired beats hunting the perfect 20%." Mental Models List versus There Is No Perfect Solution suggests "when a model lens beats treating every tradeoff as equal."

MVP versus Evergreen Notes suggests "when rough ship beats two-year polish." Slow Productivity versus The 12 Week Year suggests "when fewer deep projects beat quarterly sprints." The 12 Week Year versus Slow Productivity suggests "when quarterly intensity beats fewer deeper projects." Periodic Knowledge Review versus Minimum Effective Dose is now being drafted.

Periodic Knowledge Review versus Minimum Effective Dose now suggests "when calendar review beats the smallest sufficient habit." Weekly Review Checklists versus Minimum Effective Dose suggests "when the full checklist beats the smallest review that works." Second Brain Workflow versus Minimum Effective Dose is being drafted next.

Second Brain Workflow versus Minimum Effective Dose now suggests "when the full loop beats the smallest habit that works." Trusted Inbox versus Mobile Capture Workflows suggests "when one trusted pipe beats frictionless mobile capture everywhere." Mobile Capture versus Analog Capture suggests "when share sheet beats paper for sparks I might scroll past."

Analog Capture versus Digital Minimalism suggests "when paper wins over fewer digital pipes." Context-Aware Capture versus Metadata Strategy suggests "when inline why beats formal metadata fields." RSS for Research versus Signal vs Noise suggests "when appointment reading beats skipping every maybe-save."
</details></think>
